pages.text_excerpt: Contact Me - Toby Perkins Labour MP - Toby Perkins MP Skip to main content Toby Perkins MP Standing up for Chesterfield and Staveley Keep up to date Get in touch Donate About Me News Blogs Contact Me In Parliament My Videos More Search Search the website Search Menu Contact Me Click HERE to check if I am your MP. You can contact my team by calling 01246 386286 , e-mailing me at [email protected] or by completing the form below. You can also write to me at Chesterfield Labour Club, 113 Saltergate, Chesterfield, S40 1NF . I am asking constituents not to turn up at the office unannounced. Anyone who needs to arrange to see me or my staff should call the office and book an appointment with my team. Please make sure you include all relevant information, including your full address and contact details, when contacting me so my team and I can respond quicker. h3: Further details
pages.text_excerpt: About Me - Toby Perkins Labour MP - Toby Perkins MP Skip to main content Toby Perkins MP Standing up for Chesterfield and Staveley Keep up to date Get in touch Donate About Me News Blogs Contact Me In Parliament My Videos More Search Search the website Search Menu About Me Toby out canvassing Toby Perkins was elected as the Member of Parliament for Chesterfield in the 2010 general Election, overturning a Lib Dem majority of over 3,000 to be elected by just 549 votes. Chesterfield was only one of three gains by the Labour party at that General Election. Toby started as he meant to go on, raising the concerns of local schoolteachers at David Cameron’s first PMQs after the election. Toby was named in Ed Miliband’s Shadow Front Bench Team as a Shadow Education Minister and was the first of the new intake of MPs from the 2010 General Election to speak from the Dispatch Box. The following year Toby was appointed as Shadow Minister for Enterprise and Small Business where he was responsible for policies on Access to Finance, Small Businesses, Regulation/ de-regulation, Insolvency, Procurement, Pubs and the High Street. Toby also led opposition day debates for the party on pub company regulation, Sunday trading laws for the Olympics and on the Deregulation Bill alongside Chi Onwurah. Toby was re-elected at the 2015 General Election, with an increased majority of 13,598. In 2015 Toby was appointed as Shadow Armed Forces Minister which he held until his resignation in June 2016. On leaving the front bench, he was elected Chair of the All-Party Parliamentary Pub Group, on which he has contributed to the government’s review of the pubs code, and where he has introduced the inaugural Parliamentary Pub of the Year Award in 2018. Toby was re-elected at the 2017 General Election with a majority of 9,605. Toby is currently chair of the All-Party Parliamentary group for Tennis, and was chair of Labour Friends of the Forces from January 2016 to October 2020. In the 2019 General Election Toby was re-elected with a majority of 1,451. In 2020, following the election of Keir Starmer as leader of the Labour Party, Keir asked Toby to rejoin the Labour front bench, appointing him to the Shadow Education Team as the Shadow Minister for Apprenticeships and Lifelong Learning. In the reshuffle of December 2021 Toby’s title was changed to the Shadow Minister for Skills and Further Education. In September 2023 Toby was appointed to the shadow DEFRA team as the Shadow Minister for Nature and Rural Affairs. Toby was re-elected at the 2024 General Election, with a majority of 10,820. While Chesterfield was previously the only Labour seat outside of the cities in the whole of the East Midlands, all the seats in Derbyshire were won by Labour. Toby has sat on the Communities and Local Government, International Trade, Home Affairs and Environment, Food & Rural Affairs Select Committees and in in September 2024 Toby was elected as the Chair of the Environmental Audit Committee . Toby has spent all his adult life in Chesterfield and currently lives in the Brampton West & Loundsley Green Ward of Chesterfield Borough. Before becoming an MP Toby was a Councillor for the Rother Ward on Chesterfield Borough Council, ran his own small business supplying rugby clothing and was a Director in a social enterprise providing nursery care to one of the most deprived areas of Chesterfield. Toby at the Badger Pub on MacMillan Coffee Morning Follow me on social media Toby Perkins MP Contact details 113, Saltergate, Chesterfield, S40 1NF 113, Saltergate, Chesterfield, S40 1NF '> View on Google Maps Email: [email protected] Further details Promoted by and on behalf of the Labour Party at One Brewer's Green, London SW1H 0RH The Labour Party and its elected representatives may contact you using the information you supply. ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved.
